title: Smoky Crispy American Fettuccine Pasta
description: A smoky, crispy skillet fettuccine that combines low‑carb pasta, bacon, and a creamy smoked paprika sauce for a quick weeknight dinner.

ingredients.name: reserved pasta water
ingredients.notes: use to thin sauce if needed

instructions: Bring a large pot of salted water to a boil and cook the low‑carb fettuccine until al dente, about 9‑10 minutes. Drain, reserving 1 cup of pasta water, and set aside.

While the pasta cooks, heat a large skillet over medium heat and add the chopped bacon. Cook, stirring, until crispy, about 4‑5 minutes. Remove the bacon with a slotted spoon and set aside, leaving the rendered fat in the pan.
In the same skillet, add the olive oil to the remaining bacon fat and heat for 30 seconds.
Add the minced garlic to the skillet and sauté until fragrant, about 30 seconds.
Sprinkle the smoked paprika, salt, and black pepper over the garlic, stirring to combine, then pour in the heavy cream.
Bring the mixture to a gentle simmer, allowing the cream to thicken slightly, about 2 minutes.
Stir in the grated parmesan until melted and the sauce becomes silky.
Add the cooked fettuccine to the skillet, tossing to coat each strand with the smoky sauce. If the sauce is too thick, add a splash of the reserved pasta water to reach your desired consistency.
Fold in the crispy bacon pieces and fresh parsley, mixing everything evenly.
Transfer the skillet to the preheated broiler and broil for 3‑5 minutes, or until the top is golden and slightly crisp.
Remove from the oven and immediately sprinkle with lemon zest for a bright finish.
Serve hot, optionally garnishing with additional parmesan or parsley.
